context.env: Real Postgres: DATABASE_URL=postgres://postgres:postgres@localhost:5433/cdp (migrations applied). turbo test task is cache:false. Docker is available for LocalStack. For UI: build a runnable local app + local API adapter so Playwright can drive it.
invariants: Tenant isolation: every tenant-scoped row carries workspace_id NOT NULL; every query filters by workspace_id. Service-role Lambdas bypass RLS so they MUST scope by workspace_id in code; the admin app uses the user JWT so RLS applies.

workspace_id is NEVER from a client payload — it comes from the API-key/authorizer context or a trusted server-set field. The frontend sends the active-workspace claim via its JWT; the API resolves scope from the authorizer, never from a body field.

Role enforcement (§3A): owner/marketer/accounting are workspace-scoped; system-admin is the only cross-tenant role and every cross-tenant access is audit-logged. The UI shows only what the role permits; the API enforces it regardless of the UI.

All sends (broadcasts §9A and automation actions §9B) go through the SAME outbox -> Dispatcher pipeline (§9) and pass suppression -> frequency-cap -> quiet-hours; sending is gated on workspace active/verified.

Idempotency: broadcasts dedupe per (broadcast_id, profile_id); the automation runner must tolerate retries without double-advancing (optimistic checks / updated_at); image uploads + metering rollups are idempotent.

LOCAL-only: AWS (SES/SNS/S3/SQS) are MOCKED (aws-sdk-client-mock) or run on LocalStack; never hit real AWS. Do NOT mock Postgres in the integration tier — use the real local DB on localhost:5433. The editor must emit MJML, never hand-rolled email HTML.

Logic in pure injected functions; Lambda handlers stay thin. Reuse the prior harness (packages/db testutil: adminPool, hasDatabaseUrl, applyMigrations; runPlanInWorkspaceTx; SqlStatement {text,values}; @cdp/segments resolveAudience; @cdp/email; the dispatcher core).

Determinism: turbo test task is cache:false — keep it. Unique workspace UUIDs + file-local namespaces per integration file (events.event_id / ses_message_id are GLOBAL keys). No cross-file races. Frontend e2e (Playwright) must be deterministic (seeded data, stable selectors).
Scope discipline: implement exactly what the cited sections require for THIS phase — no features beyond the spec. Keep the cost model EXACTLY as §20 (direct usage + even split of fixed costs).
context.loop: Backend phase: pure unit tests + DB-integration against REAL local Postgres (localhost:5433); mock AWS (aws-sdk-client-mock) / LocalStack. No frontend.

criteria: An enrolled profile advances through trigger -> wait -> condition -> action -> exit.
A wait defers until next_run_at; a branch (condition) routes correctly using the §8 compiler against profile/features/membership.
The runner is idempotent (no double-advance on retry / concurrent sweep).
Automation action sends pass through the Dispatcher guards (suppression/cap/quiet-hours); enrollment via segment entry works; re-enrollment policy is enforced. Everything workspace-scoped.
scope: A automation DSL: automations.definition is a graph of nodes [trigger|wait|condition|action|exit] + edges. Define the node schema in this phase.
Enrollment: a trigger (segment entry via segment_change_log, an event, or manual) inserts a automation_enrollments row at the start node (UNIQUE (automation_id, profile_id)).

services/automation-runner: scheduled sweep WHERE status=active AND next_run_at <= now(); process the current node: wait -> set next_run_at; condition -> evaluate via the §8 compiler, pick next node, process immediately; action(send) -> insert outbox row -> Dispatcher; exit -> completed.
Idempotent advance (tolerate retries without double-advancing — use updated_at / optimistic checks). Define and implement the re-enrollment policy.
phase.specSections: §9B (table-driven per-profile state machine: nodes trigger/wait/condition/action/exit; Automation-runner sweep on next_run_at; enrollment via segment entry), §8 (reuse compiler for branch conditions), §9 (Dispatcher for action sends), §6 (automations, automation_enrollments, segment_change_log)
phase.title: Automation workflow engine

implUnits: services/automation-runner/src/core.ts pure decisions + SqlStatement builders (reuse @cdp/segments buildSegmentMatch + broadcast enqueue contract)
services/automation-runner/src/enroll.ts enrollment orchestrator (parse change_log -> decideReenrollment -> insert in workspace tx)
services/automation-runner/src/run.ts runEnrollment: claim CAS -> process node chain single tick to wait/exit boundary -> enqueue sends -> advance; workspace_id from row
services/automation-runner/src/handler.ts thin sweep handler isolate per-row failures
mocks: SES via injected SesEmailClient CountingSes through real dispatchOutbox
SQS via aws-sdk-client-mock / injected sender ({outbox_id} only)
EventBridge NOT tested (test sweep query + advance directly)
plan.notes: Schema exists (0005): automations + automation_enrollments UNIQUE(automation_id,profile_id) + (status,next_run_at) index — no migration. DSL: definition={startNode, nodes:{id:Node}}; Node=trigger{kind:segment_entry|event|manual,next}|wait{delay{seconds}|ISO8601|until,next}|condition{ast:§8,onTrue,onFalse}|action{kind:send|set_attribute,template_id?/key?/value?,next}|exit. Re-enrollment 'once' default (structural ON CONFLICT (automation_id,profile_id) DO NOTHING + decideReenrollment); reenroll_after_exit specified but off (UNIQUE has no status component; true re-entry needs versioned key, documented). Idempotent advance = CAS on updated_at (UPDATE WHERE updated_at=$expected RETURNING) so concurrent sweeps/retries advance at most once; action sends also rely on outbox dedupe_key + dispatcher atomic claim for exactly-once SEND. Runner re-implements NONE of suppression/cap/quiet — all automation sends via real dispatchOutbox. Sweep cross-workspace (each row carries workspace_id loaded from row never assumed); every WRITE workspace-scoped via runStatementsInWorkspaceTx. Mock SES+SQS at boundary; real PG for integration gated hasDatabaseUrl/skipIf. Don't unit-test scheduler fired. Reuse runStatementsInWorkspaceTx (dispatcher/broadcast) not runPlanInWorkspaceTx for these writes.

assertions: validateAutomationDefinition: one trigger, unique ids, resolvable edges, reachable exit
rejects no/2+ trigger, dangling edge, dup id
rejects unknown node/trigger types
wait requires exactly one of delay|until
condition requires §8 AST + onTrue + onFalse
action send requires template_id; set_attribute requires key+value
resolveStartNode/findNode
trigger -> advance to next immediately
wait{delay} -> stay nextRunAt=now+delay
wait{until future} -> nextRunAt=until stay
wait{until past} -> advance
condition matchesNow true->onTrue false->onFalse process immediately
action send -> advance + sideEffect enqueue_send
exit -> complete status=completed
pure
computeWaitNextRunAt {seconds} and ISO-8601 duration
until returns absolute date
isWaitElapsed boundary-inclusive
throws if no delay/until
buildBranchMatchQuery delegates to @cdp/segments buildSegmentMatch, workspace_id values[0], AND p.id=$
unknown field/operator throws (whitelist)
non-empty match->onTrue empty->onFalse
null AST -> match-all -> onTrue
buildEnrollmentClaim CAS WHERE workspace_id=$1 AND id=$2 AND status=active AND updated_at=$3 RETURNING
buildAdvanceEnrollment sets current_node/next_run_at/status/updated_at guarded on prior updated_at, workspace_id $1
throws falsy workspaceId
buildSweepQuery status=active AND next_run_at<=now cross-workspace
RETURNING distinguishes winner/loser
buildEnrollmentInsert ON CONFLICT (automation_id,profile_id) DO NOTHING current_node=start status=active workspace_id $1
decideReenrollment 'once' blocks while any prior exists
parseEnrollmentTrigger maps entered+segment_id to enrollment intent for automation with matching trigger_segment_id; exited -> none
dedupe (automation_id,profile_id)
buildAutomationDedupeKey(automationId,profileId,nodeId) stable unique
buildAutomationOutboxInsert ON CONFLICT (dedupe_key) DO NOTHING automation_id set workspace_id $1
buildDispatchEnqueueMessage {outbox_id} only no workspace_id
enqueue-only: no suppression/cap/quiet re-implemented
trigger->wait in one tick: claim, process trigger, land wait, next_run_at=now+delay, SQS not called
later tick wait->condition->action: evaluate branch, enqueue one send, advance to exit
exit -> status=completed clear next_run_at not re-swept
condition->immediate chains multiple non-wait nodes single tick
loop-guard MAX_STEPS_PER_TICK
sweep then runEnrollment per row; lost CAS claim skipped no-op
throwing enrollment isolated, others advance
thin wrapper, logic in injected core
real PG: automation trigger->wait->condition->action(send)->exit; enroll; tick1 lands wait next_run_at=now+delay
advance clock + sweep: condition evals real profile_features via §8 compiler, routes action, one outbox row automation_id status=pending
real dispatchOutbox (SES mocked): SES once with config_set, messages_log with automation_id, outbox sent
final tick exit -> status=completed next_run_at NULL not re-swept
sweep doesn't return enrollment while now<next_run_at
no advance/outbox while wait pending
once now>=next_run_at sweep returns + advances; deferral by real sweep query/index
purchase_30d>=3: profile w/5 -> onTrue, w/1 -> onFalse vs real profile_features via buildSegmentMatch
branch scoped workspace_id $1 + AND p.id=$profile
onTrue/onFalse target written to current_node
two concurrent runEnrollment on same due enrollment: CAS on updated_at -> exactly one wins advances, loser no-op
advances exactly one step no double-advance
due action under concurrent sweep -> one outbox row (ON CONFLICT dedupe_key)
replay same tick after complete -> no-op (stale updated_at matches no row)
segment_change_log entered+segment_id=S -> one enrollment at start node workspace-scoped
second entered same (automation,P) -> no row (ON CONFLICT)
exited -> no enrollment
other workspace's segment never enrolls here
automation status!=active -> no enroll
action send -> outbox -> real dispatchOutbox passes gate->suppression->cap->quiet before SES
suppressed recipient -> automation send SKIPPED SES not called
workspace not active/verified -> refused at gate
clean send -> messages_log automation_id + usage emails_sent +1
runner never calls SES
every write binds workspace_id $1 (runStatementsInWorkspaceTx asserts values[0])
two workspaces same automation shape + same external_id advance independently no cross-bleed
cross-workspace sweep claims each row under its own workspace_id from the row
cross-phase: segment entry->enrollment->sweep->wait->branch(§8)->action->real dispatchOutbox->messages_log driving actual cores
happy path: status=completed, one messages_log automation_id, outbox sent, workspace config_set on send
isolation peer workspace unaffected

instructions: Read CDP-BUILD-SPEC.md sections: §9B (table-driven per-profile state machine: nodes trigger/wait/condition/action/exit; Automation-runner sweep on next_run_at; enrollment via segment entry), §8 (reuse compiler for branch conditions), §9 (Dispatcher for action sends), §6 (automations, automation_enrollments, segment_change_log).
FIRST write the failing tests from the plan (red), then implement to green. Logic in pure injected functions; handlers/components thin.
Honor every invariant — tenant isolation, role enforcement, idempotency, and "all sends go through the Dispatcher guards" are pass/fail.

Mock AWS (aws-sdk-client-mock) or use LocalStack; integration tier uses the REAL local Postgres (never mock the DB). For UI phases, verify with Playwright in a real browser against the locally-running app + local API; seed deterministic data.

Run the relevant `pnpm test` (with DATABASE_URL), plus Playwright e2e for UI phases, `pnpm typecheck`, `pnpm lint`. Iterate until GREEN including a COLD turbo run. Confirm no regression in earlier phases. Report actual command output.
Implement ONLY what the cited sections require. Keep the §20 cost model exact. The editor must emit MJML, not hand-rolled HTML.
prompt.outputFormat: JSON: { filesCreated: string[], filesModified: string[], testsPass: boolean, testSummary: string, e2eSummary: string, typecheckOk: boolean, lintOk: boolean, commandsRun: string[], notes: string }
prompt.role: senior full-stack TypeScript engineer building a serverless multi-tenant CDP + Preact SPA, test-first
prompt.task: Implement "Automation workflow engine" to satisfy its test plan and §18 criteria. Red -> green -> refactor.

Idempotent advance (tolerate retries without double-advancing — use updated_at / optimistic checks). Define and implement the re-enrollment policy.
